name: sqlmodel3
documentation:
# sqlmodel3

sqlmodel的国产化替代品，用法和sqlmodel完全一样！！！

## 安装

```shell
pip install sqlmodel3
```

classes.name: sqlmodel3.sql._expression_select_cls.SelectBase

methods.name: sqlmodel3.sql._expression_select_cls.SelectBase.where
documentation:
Return a new `Select` construct with the given expression added to
its `WHERE` clause, joined to the existing clause via `AND`, if any.

methods.name: sqlmodel3.sql._expression_select_cls.SelectBase.having
documentation:
Return a new `Select` construct with the given expression added to
its `HAVING` clause, joined to the existing clause via `AND`, if any.
Separate this class in SelectBase, Select, and SelectOfScalar so that they can share
where and having without having type overlap incompatibility in session.exec().

classes.name: sqlmodel3.sql._expression_select_cls.SelectOfScalar
documentation: This is not comparable to sqlalchemy3.sql.selectable.ScalarSelect, that has a different
purpose. This is the same as a normal SQLAlchemy Select class where there's only one
entity, so the result will be converted to a scalar by default. This way writing
for loops on the results will feel natural.

functions.name: sqlmodel3.sql.expression.all_
documentation: Redefine operatos that would only take a column expresion to also take the (virtual)
types of Pydantic models, e.g. str instead of only Mapped[str].

modules.name: sqlmodel3.sql._expression_select_gen
documentation: WARNING: do not modify this code, it is generated by _expression_select_gen.py.jinja2

methods.name: sqlmodel3.orm.session.Session.execute
documentation:
🚨 You probably want to use `session.exec()` instead of `session.execute()`.

This is the original SQLAlchemy `session.execute()` method that returns objects
of type `Row`, and that you have to call `scalars()` to get the model objects.

For example:

```Python
heroes = session.execute(select(Hero)).scalars().all()
```

instead you could use `exec()`:

```Python
heroes = session.exec(select(Hero)).all()
```

methods.name: sqlmodel3.orm.session.Session.query
documentation:
🚨 You probably want to use `session.exec()` instead of `session.query()`.

`session.exec()` is SQLModel's own short version with increased type
annotations.

Or otherwise you might want to use `session.execute()` instead of
`session.query()`.

functions.name: sqlmodel3.default.Default
documentation:
You shouldn't use this function directly.

It's used internally to recognize when a default value has been overwritten, even
if the overridden default value was truthy.

classes.name: sqlmodel3.default._DefaultPlaceholder
documentation:
You shouldn't use this class directly.

It's used internally to recognize when a default value has been overwritten, even
if the overridden default value was truthy.

methods.name: sqlmodel3.ext.asyncio.session.AsyncSession.execute
documentation:
🚨 You probably want to use `session.exec()` instead of `session.execute()`.

This is the original SQLAlchemy `session.execute()` method that returns objects
of type `Row`, and that you have to call `scalars()` to get the model objects.

For example:

```Python
heroes = await session.execute(select(Hero)).scalars().all()
```

instead you could use `exec()`:

```Python
heroes = await session.exec(select(Hero)).all()
```

functions.name: sqlmodel3.main.Field
documentation: When foreign_key is str, include ondelete

methods.name: sqlmodel3.main.SQLModelMetaclass.__init__
documentation: Override SQLAlchemy, allow both SQLAlchemy and plain Pydantic models

methods.name: sqlmodel3.main.SQLModel._calculate_keys
documentation: From Pydantic, override to only show keys from fields, omit SQLAlchemy attributes

classes.name: sqlmodel3._compat.ModelField
documentation: Dummy for types, to make it importable
